lines.text: Central Perk, everyone except Phoebe is there. Bonnie is telling them of her sex-capades.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Bonnie) So ah, your first sexual experience was with a woman?!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: All right, I was 15, it was my best friend, Ruth, and we got drunk on that hard cider, and then suddenly, I don't know, we were, we were making out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell it again. (pause, we see Rachel is not amused.) Seriously.
[cut to Rachel and Monica at the counter.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) I mean is that woman capable of talking about anything else but sex?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, sure. Well y'know, earlier she was talking about geography.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, she was listing the countries she's done it in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I think we all learned something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering, hurridly) Hey, you guys! Look what I found! Look at this! (She hands Chandler a picture) That's my Mom's writing! Look.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (reading the back of the picture) Me and Frank and Phoebe, Graduation 1965.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know what that means?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That you're actually 50?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, that's not, that's not me Phoebe, that's her pal Phoebe. According to her high school yearbook, they were like B.F.F. (Ross and Bonnie look at her quizzically) Best Friends Forever.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is so cool.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! So this woman probably could like have all kinds of stories about my parents, and she might even know like where my Dad is. So I looked her up, and she lives out by the beach. So maybe this weekend we could go to the beach?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah, we can!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: (to Ross) Shoot! I can't go, I have to work!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's too bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sarcastic) Ohh, big, fat bummerrr.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So great! Okay! Tomorrow we're gonna drive out to Montauk.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Bonnie had sex there!
Rachel turns and gives him a look, and Joey quickly apologises.
Opening Credits
Outside Central Perk, Chandler and Monica are waiting for Phoebe to arrive with the cab.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (watching a happy couple walk by, arm in arm) Would you look at them. Am I ever gonna find a boyfriend again? I gonna die an old maid.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're not gonna die an old maid, maybe an old spinster cook.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sarcastic) Thanks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey now besides, if worst comes to worst, I'll be your boyfriend.
At that suggestion Monica starts laughing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why is that so funny?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You made a joke right? So I laughed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ha-ha-ha. A little to hard. What am I not ah, boyfriend material?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, no. You're Chandler. Y'know, Chandler! (hits him on the arm)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so we've established my name, and hit me. But theoretically y'know, I mean say we weren't friends, say it's a blind date. I show up at your door, and I'm like (in a fake voice) "Hey, nice to meet, ya. Hey, oh-hey."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I'd probably be scared of a guy using a fake voice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (walking up carrying a brown paper bag) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, hey! Oh good, you brought food!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, it's just my luggage.
Phoebe drives up.

lines.speaker: Chandler, Monica, and Joey
lines.text: Hey!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woo-hoo! All right! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I am having the best karma this week. First, I find this woman who knew my parents, and then my client with the fuzzy back gives me his beach house.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? What about ah, that bike messenger you hit?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I wasn't talking about his karma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (approaching) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, check out the hat!
She is wearing this giant straw hat, the brim on it must be at least, least foot wide.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a minute, I know that hat! I was taken aboard that hat! They did experiments on me! I can't have children!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Seriously, where did you get the hat?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross gave it to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I think she looks good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Buy it for ya, or win it for ya?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well excuse me, my fashion-impaired friends, I am here to tell you that hats are back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And this time, they've ganged up to form one giant, super hat.
At the Beach, it's raining cats and dogs as the gang arrives. Chandler and Monica are taking shelter under Rachel's hat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Go, go, go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, now everybody wants to be under this hat!
They get inside and notice on small problem.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oy!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's with all this sand? (picking a handful of sand off of the floor, which is covered in sand)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, Bob said there might be flood damage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, either that, or he has a really big cat.
Phoebe Sr. house, she is a real estate agent and is trying to sell a house over the phone. By the way, it's still raining outside.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Well, yes, it's kind've an unusual house. It has umm, three beautiful bedrooms and ah, no baths. But y'know, the ocean is right there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (at the door) Knock, knock, knock.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: (on phone) Ah, oh, hang on a second. (to Phoebe) Come in, come in. (on phone) All right, so think about it, and call me back. (hangs up)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Are you ah, Phoebe Abott?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Ahh, yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi Phoebe Abott, I'm your best friends daughter!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: You're Erwin's daughter?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I-I mean your-your old best friend, here. (hands her the picture) Lily, from high school. Remember?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Oh gosh, Lily, yes. Of course I remember Lily. I... Then you must be?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (points to herself) Phoebe. (points to her) Phoebe. Phoebe, yeah. She named me after you I guess.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Uh-huh. Wow! Well, look! There's Frank. (points to the picture.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes!! Yes! Yes! Yes!! That's my Dad, that's Frank! Yeah! I'm sorry I'm getting all flingy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Take it easy--if you want, there's cookies on the counter, or, or--sangria! (jumps up) I can make sangria!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, sorry. Cookies are good, thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (goes and gets some cookies) Well, so, umm, anyway umm, I've been, I've been looking for my Father, and umm, have you heard from him, or seen him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Oh no, I-I'm sorry, I guess we lost track of everybody after high school.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (disappointed) Oh, okay. Well, so tell me everything about my parents. Everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Ohh, well. Y'know we were always together, in fact the had a nickname for the three of us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, what? What was it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: The three losers. Oh, poor Lily. (Phoebe notices a picture on the fridge, takes it, and puts it in her pocket.) Ohh, y'know I-I heard about what happened, that must have been just terrible for you, losing your mother that way.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (happily) Yeah, no, it was great.
The beach house, it's still raining. Chandler is building a sand castle, Rachel is doing Monica's nails, and they're all drinking margaritas, obviously bored.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (getting an idea) Hey, y'know what a really good rainy day game is?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean naked game. Strip poker, we should totally play strip poker.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No, no!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you crazy?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! When you go away, you-you have to play, it's like a law!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica) Allll done!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, who's next?! (She looks around the room, and stops when she comes to Ross.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-o-o! (Rachel gives him a "Please?" look.) No way!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, please?! I'm boredddd! You let me do it once before.
Ross shoots Joey a look, who shoots Chandler a look, who gives Joey an "Oh my God." look back.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah well, if ah, if that's the rule this weekend... (She gets up) No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! (she starts creeping up on him)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get away!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just once!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Stay away!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Take it like a man, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he gets up and starts to run away from her) No! (in his escape attempt he crushes Chandler's sand castle) No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Big bullies!!
Ross dives over the couch, Rachel goes the other way, and lands up top of him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ow! Ow! Oh, no-no-no!
They get into a wrestling match, that ends with Ross making Rachel paint her forehead with the nail polish. They both end up lying next to each other, stop, and look at each other for a moment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Oh, hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, so, how are we doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bored and bored!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you know what naked card game is never boring?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Noo!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) So what's Phoebe like?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm kind, caring, and sweet. What's Monica like?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah no, the other Phoebe, the one you went to go see.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, I think she knows where my Dad is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh well, where is he?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She was acting, she was pretending like she hasn't heard from him on years, but I found this picture on her fridge, and look (shows Monica)! Isn't this what he would look like now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gasps) Totally familiar. (Phoebe shows the rest of them.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: The Guys
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, why would she lie to you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know, but we're having dinner tomorrow night, so I figured, she's gonna tell me then. Y'know maybe she just wanted to give him time to, buy me presents, I don't know! So, you're all bored?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I'm gonna close my eyes and point to someone, and you, whoever I point has to come up with something fun for us to do, and we have to do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, all right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fan out! Fan out!
They do so, and Phoebe gets in the middle, closes her eyes, and starts spinning in a circle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Starts to spin) Ooh, y'know we could just do this. (She stops at Chandler)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, umm, we all have to play strip poker.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (jumping in triumph) OH YES!!!!!
[cut to later]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Strip Happy Days Game?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, well, I couldn't find any cards, so it was either this or Strip Bag Of Old Knitting Stuff.
Monica rolls, and Ross goes first.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, (reading the card) Fonzy gives you two thumbs up, collect two cool points. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica, if you get five cool points, you get to make somebody take off one item of clothing. It hasn't happened yet, but we're all very excited.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, come on! (blows on the dice) Daddy needs a new pair of electromagnetic microscopes for the Prehistoric Forensics Department! (They all look at him, and he shuts up and rolls the dice.) (he moves his piece) Okay. (reading a card) Take Pinky Tuscadero up to Inspiration Point, collect three cool points!! Yeah! Which gives me five, and let's see who is gonna lose their clothes. Ummmm, I think I pick our strip poker sponsor Mr. Joey Tribianni.

lines.speaker: The Girls
lines.text: Woo-hooooo!!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, relax. It's just a shoe.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Wooooo!!!!
[cut to later in the game]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (reading a card) Okay, your band is playing at Arnold's, collect three cool points. Which means, I have five, and that means I get Joey's boxers!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine. Gang up on me! I got you all right where I want you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, take 'em off!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually, y'know it's kinda cold, so how about I keep my boxers on, and give you all a peek at the good stuff?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (getting up) All right, I'm gonna make more margaritas! (She pours the rest of the pitcher into Ross's glass.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa, hey! What are doing? Trying to get me drunk?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm just making margaritas.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think I'll help her out. (She gets up to go over and help Rachel, and reveals she has no pants.) (to Rachel) What is going on here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You painting his toenails?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chasing him all around the room?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica, please?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's totally flirting with you too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pause) He is, isn't he? I don't know, I don't know, I mean maybe it's just being here at the beach together or, I don't know. But it's like something... (she's interrupted by the sound of Bonnie entering)

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! (Rachel is shocked) Hi Bonnie!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Hi! My boss let me off early, so I took the train.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: What are you guys doing?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We're playing Strip Happy Days Game!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Cool! I'll catch up! (She takes off her sweater.)
Commercial Break
The beach house, the next morning. Chandler and Monica are in the kitchen eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, you still don't think I'm boyfriend material?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I saw you checking me out during the game last night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn't even take off your pants.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, lucky for you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Well! Is everybody else having just the best time?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shhh! Shhhh! Joey's asleep.
Joey is sleeping on the floor and is buried in sand that has been carved into a mermaid complete with breasts.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: After he passed out, we put the sand around him to keep him warm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I assume the ah, happy couple isn't up yet. Did you guys hear them last night?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah, I don't know what they were doing, but at one point sea turtles actually came up to the house.
Ross and Bonnie enter

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Hey! How did everybody sleep?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Like a log.

lines.speaker: Ross and Bonnie
lines.text: Us too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm going for a walk.
Joey finally wakes up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Joey) Good morning. Nice breasts by the way.
Joey looks down and his look turns from shock to satisfaction.
The porch, Bonnie is coming back from swimming, Rachel is reading.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Hey, what happened to you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, ah nothin'. I just felt like hangin' out here and reading.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Oh, the water was sooo great! We jumped off this pier and my suit came off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhhh, sorry I missed that.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Yeah, Joey and Chandler sure are funny.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh-ha-ha!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: I think I brought back half of the beach in my hair. It was so much easier when I used to shave my head.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, I gotta tell ya, I just loved your look when you were bald.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Because I think about shaving it all off again sometime.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean you definitely should do that.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Y'know what, I should do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Yeah, thank you Rachel, you are soo cool.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awww, stop. Come on. Now go shave that head!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: All right.
As Bonnie goes to do just that, Rachel smiles to herself, proud of what she's done.
The beach house, at night. Phoebe is hangs up the phone, and gently pushes one of the stools over.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the matter, Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She cancelled! My namesake cancelled on me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, she clamed she had to go out of town suddenly. She's avoiding me, she doesn't want to tell me where my Father is. She knows, and she won't tell me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aww Pheebs, that sucks!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well, don't "Aww Pheebs, that sucks!" me yet. (she starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where ya going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, she's out of town so, there's gotta be something in her house that tells me where my Father is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Pheebs, some people call that breaking and entering.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, are any of those people here?!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh, no!! No, no!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, look I-I-I do something nice, okay? I'll-I'll fill her ice trays.
She exits just as Bonnie comes down the stairs, as bald as Michael Jordan.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Hey, everybody!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Wow!! (they all recoil in shock and horror)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wh-haa-haa! Look what 'cha did! (Rachel has her hand over her mouth to keep from laughing.)

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: You wanna touch it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nooo, but it, but it's great.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Come on, touch it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (He gently touches it.) You can feel all the bones in your skull.
Outside the beach house, Ross is coming out to talk to Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was having a little chat with ah, Bonnie, and ah, guess what, she-she happened to bring up y'know, who was behind the um, whole head shaving idea, and now, who was it? Oh, that's right, that's right, it was you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That was her idea, I just gave her a nudge.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She said you gave her the razor!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: (joining them) Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: So, anyone up for a midnight dip in the ocean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh, no, I'm good.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you in a bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, have fun! Wooo!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on see, she doesn't look that bad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You can see the moonlight bouncing off her head! What the hell were you thinking?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You don't know?! Rach, you balded my girlfriend!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right! Ross, do you think it's easy for me to see you with somebody else?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, hey! You're the one who ended it, remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, because I was mad at you, not because I stopped loving you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You still love me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You still love me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y-yeah, so, you-you love me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Noo, nnnnn. What does this mean? What do you, I mean do you wanna, get back together?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo! Maybe! I, I don't know. Ross, I still can't forgive you for what you did, I can't, I just, but sometimes when I'm with you I just, I feel so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just, I feel, I-I just...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I feel...
Ross leans in and kisses her. They both look at each other for a moment, and then embrace in a more passionate kiss, only to be interrupted by Joey and Chandler coming outside.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) Noo!! I don't care! I'm not, I'm not gonna playing one-on-one strip poker with you for practice!
Rachel and Ross both stop kissing, and quickly step back from each other.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But I made cards!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well! Good night. (to Ross) I'm going upstairs.

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: G'night.
Rachel walks inside, stops, and turns back to look at Ross for a moment then goes upstairs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross) Wanna play strip poker for practice?
Phoebe Sr.'s house, Phoebe is breaking in through a window.
She throws her bag inside, and starts to climb through the window. She gets halfway in and the window slams shut on her butt.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ow! My ass. Okay. Okay. (She manages to climb completely inside and the window slams shut.) Oh, shhh!

She starts walking across the darkened room and hits her head on a wind chime hanging from the ceiling, to stop it from making a noise she grabs it and "Shhh's" it. She goes into the kitchen and finds Phoebe Sr.'s appointment book, to read it she opens the freezer. Just as she starts to read, Phoebe Sr. jumps out from her bedroom with a coat hanger, startling Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No!! It's me! It's me! I-I didn't want to make any noise!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Then don't break in!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: What are you doing here?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I, came to fill your ice cube trays.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, okay, okay, look. I took this picture from your fridge. Okay, because I know that this is my Father. Yeah, this is Frank Buffay and you are standing right there next to him. Now, look I deserve to know where I came from. All right? So if you can help me find my Father then you should! Otherwise, you're just mean! (pause) So, just tell me the truth!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: All right, the man in the picture is Chuck Magioni.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My Father is Chuck Magioni?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: No, no, that's just Chuck Magioni, I-I sold him a house last year! And I'm very sorry, but I don't know where your Father is, and that's the truth.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: But umm, you're right. I think that a person should know where they come from. Wh-which is why I ah, (pause) ahh, (pause) okay. I'm your mother.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Heh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Y'know I wanted to tell you yesterday, but I just, I kinda felt all floopy, and...
At that Phoebe's eyes open in shock.
Outside the beach house, Ross is telling Joey and Chandler what happened with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm telling ya, you guys are totally getting back together!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's not true! Her, she doesn't even know what she wants! Rachel's still mad about the whole thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, then you gotta back away, all right? You don't need that kind've hurt. Take it from a guy who's never had a long term relationship......

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, but ahhhhhh!! I really wanna go up there and finish that kiss!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: (coming back from her swim) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahhhh! (Steps away from her.)

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: You guys, the water's great. You should really go in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, ahh, no thanks, I just had an M&M.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Okay, well g'night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good night.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: (kisses Ross) Don't be too long.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okey-dokey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There is not one hair on that head.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, it'll grow back, right? And she-she's really fun, and she's cool, and-and I'm finally moving on. Y'know? I mean getting over Rachel was so (makes an incoherent nasal sound), y'know? Y'know, and I'm finally feeling sane again. And now if I go up there, and-and I kiss her, and, Gooood I wanna kiss her, and-and-and it doesn't work out, right? Do I really wanna put myself through that again?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So let me get this straight. If you go with Bonnie tonight, you're doing the smart, healthy thing and moving on.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right, and you go with Rachel, Bonnie's free tonight?

The hallway between the bedrooms. Ross is coming upstairs and stops between two doors. He looks at the one on the right, then he looks at the one on his left, thinks about it, and goes in the one on his right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering the bedroom) Hi. (closes the door)
Closing Credits
The beach house, Chandler is simulating he's coming to pick up Monica for a date. Chandler knocks on the door, and Monica answers it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (in a fake voice) Hi there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's that weird voice again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Let me try it again, you're gonna wanna date this next guy, I swear!
Monica closes the door, Chandler knocks, and Monica opens it to reveal Chandler on his knees.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi! I'm Dorf! You're date for the evening. (Monica walks away in disgust) Oh come on! Dorf on dating, that's good stuff!!
End
Season 3
